text:
Bank Account Claims After Death (India — RBI Guidelines):

OVERVIEW:
When an account holder passes away, the bank account must be either transferred to the nominee/legal heir or closed. The process depends on whether a nominee exists, the account balance, and the type of account.

SCENARIO A: Nominee Exists
STEP 1: Visit the bank branch where the account is held
STEP 2: Inform the branch manager about the death and request the "Claim Form for Deceased Account Holder"
STEP 3: Submit the following documents:
  - Death certificate (certified copy)
  - Nominee's identity proof (Aadhaar + PAN)
  - Nominee's bank account details (for fund transfer)
  - Original passbook or last statement
  - Duly filled claim/nomination form
STEP 4: Bank verifies documents (7-15 working days)
STEP 5: Funds transferred to nominee's account or demand draft issued
- Timeline: 15-30 working days
- Link: https://www.rbi.org.in/

SCENARIO B: No Nominee — Balance ≤ ₹5,00,000
Per RBI Master Circular, banks MUST settle claims without insisting on succession certificate:
STEP 1: Visit the branch and explain the situation
STEP 2: Submit:
  - Death certificate (certified copy)
  - Letter of Indemnity (provided by bank)
  - Affidavit on stamp paper (as per bank format)
  - Claimant's KYC (Aadhaar + PAN)
  - Relationship proof (ration card, family ID, or affidavit)
STEP 3: Bank may ask for surety from another account holder
STEP 4: Settlement within 15-30 working days
- Important: RBI mandates that banks should NOT insist on legal heir certificate or succession certificate for amounts up to ₹5 lakh.

SCENARIO C: No Nominee — Balance > ₹5,00,000
STEP 1: Obtain Legal Heir Certificate from Tehsildar/SDM OR Succession Certificate from Civil Court
  - Legal Heir Certificate: Apply at Tehsildar office with death certificate, family details, and two witnesses. Processing: 15-30 days. Cost: ₹50-100.
  - Succession Certificate: Apply in Civil Court under Indian Succession Act. Processing: 2-6 months. Cost: Court fees + lawyer fees.
STEP 2: Submit to bank along with:
  - Death certificate
  - Claimant's KYC
  - Bank claim form
  - Original passbook
STEP 3: Bank processes claim (15-30 working days after document verification)

JOINT ACCOUNT:
- If account is "Either or Survivor" or "Former or Survivor": The surviving holder can continue operating the account. Submit death certificate to update records.
- If account is "Joint": The surviving holder(s) need to follow the claim process above.

FIXED DEPOSITS (FD):
- Nominee exists: FD can be prematurely withdrawn or transferred. No penalty for premature closure due to death.
- No nominee: Same process as Scenario B or C above.

RECURRING DEPOSITS (RD):
- Can be prematurely closed with death certificate + nominee/legal heir documents.
- Matured RDs: Claim directly with documents.

SAFE DEPOSIT LOCKER:
- Nominee/legal heir can access the locker in the presence of bank officials.
- Contents inventoried and handed over.
- Locker rent must be settled.

BANK-SPECIFIC CONTACTS:
- SBI: Visit home branch, call 1800-425-3800
  Link: https://www.onlinesbi.sbi/
- HDFC Bank: Call 1800-266-6161, visit branch
  Link: https://www.hdfcbank.com/
- ICICI Bank: Call 1800-200-3344
  Link: https://www.icicibank.com/
- PNB: Call 1800-180-2222
  Link: https://www.pnbindia.in/
- Bank of Baroda: Call 1800-258-4455
  Link: https://www.bankofbaroda.in/

ESCALATION:
- If bank delays beyond 30 days: File complaint with Banking Ombudsman
  Link: https://cms.rbi.org.in/
- RBI Helpline: 14448
- Written complaint to Regional Office of RBI

REQUIRED DOCUMENTS CHECKLIST:
1. Death certificate (certified copy) × 2
2. Claimant's Aadhaar card
3. Claimant's PAN card
4. Bank account passbook or last statement
5. Claim form (bank-specific)
6. Letter of Indemnity (if no nominee, balance ≤ ₹5L)
7. Legal Heir Certificate / Succession Certificate (if no nominee, balance > ₹5L)
8. Relationship proof
9. Cancelled cheque of claimant's account (for fund transfer)

Death Certificate Process (India):

OVERVIEW:
A death certificate is the most critical document required for all post-death administrative tasks. It is legally required for bank claims, insurance, property transfer, pension, and government benefits. Without it, almost no financial or legal process can begin.

STEP 1: Obtain Medical Certificate of Cause of Death (MCCD)
- If death occurred in a hospital: The attending doctor issues the MCCD automatically.
- If death occurred at home: Call the local municipal authority or nearest government hospital. A registered medical practitioner must certify the cause of death.
- If death occurred due to accident/unnatural causes: File an FIR with the police. The police will arrange post-mortem, and the hospital will issue the MCCD after post-mortem.
- Required: Identity proof of deceased, hospital records (if any)
- Timeline: Usually same day or next day

STEP 2: Register the Death
- Registration must be done within 21 days of death at the local municipal corporation / gram panchayat office.
- Late registration (after 21 days but within 30 days): Allowed with a late fee.
- Late registration (after 30 days): Requires a Magistrate's order.
- WHO registers: The head of the household, nearest relative, or hospital authority (if institutional death).
- Portal for online registration (varies by state):
  - Delhi: https://edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in/
  - Maharashtra: https://aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in/
  - Karnataka: https://kaveri2.karnataka.gov.in/
  - Tamil Nadu: https://cnr.tn.gov.in/
  - General: Check your state's e-District portal

STEP 3: Required Documents for Registration
- MCCD (Medical Certificate of Cause of Death)
- Proof of identity of deceased (Aadhaar, Voter ID, Passport)
- Proof of identity of informant/applicant
- Proof of address of place of death
- Hospital discharge summary (if applicable)
- FIR copy (if unnatural death)

STEP 4: Obtain Death Certificate
- After registration, the Registrar issues the Death Certificate.
- Processing time: 7-15 working days (varies by state and municipality)
- Collect multiple certified copies (at least 8-10 copies recommended)
- Each copy may cost ₹10-50 depending on the state
- Online application available in many states through e-District portals

STEP 5: Certified Copies
- Get at least 8-10 certified copies — you will need them for:
  - Each bank account claim
  - Insurance claims
  - EPF/PF withdrawal
  - Property mutation
  - Pension claims
  - Utility account closure
  - Vehicle transfer
  - Court proceedings (if any)

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- If the death occurred abroad: The Indian Embassy/Consulate will assist. The foreign death certificate must be apostilled and translated.
- If no MCCD is available: A declaration by the head of the family and two witnesses may be accepted in some states.
- Keep the original death certificate safe. Always submit certified copies, not the original.
- Some institutions accept self-attested photocopies; others require notarized or gazetted officer-attested copies.

ESCALATION:
- If the municipal office delays beyond 30 days: File an RTI application or approach the District Registrar.
- For grievances: Contact the Chief Registrar of Births and Deaths in your state.
- Online grievance portals are available in most states.

TIMELINE SUMMARY:
- MCCD: 1-2 days
- Registration: Within 21 days (free), 21-30 days (with fee), after 30 days (Magistrate order)
- Death Certificate issuance: 7-15 working days after registration
- Recommended: Start within 3-5 days of death

Digital Account Management After Death (India):

BANK ACCOUNTS (RBI Guidelines + Major Banks):
  Accounts ≤₹5L, no nominee: indemnity bond only (no succession cert required per RBI Master Circular)
  Docs: death cert ×3, claimant KYC, indemnity bond, passbook
  Processing: 7–15 working days
  Joint account survivor: death cert only
  Nominee: ID + death cert + claim form → 15 days
  Above ₹5L: Legal Heir Certificate or Succession Certificate
  Escalation: Banking Ombudsman if >30 days
  SBI: visit home branch, request "Claim Form for Deceased Account"
    Link: https://www.onlinesbi.sbi/
  HDFC Bank: call 1800-266-6161
    Link: https://www.hdfcbank.com/
  ICICI Bank: call 1800-200-3344
    Link: https://www.icicibank.com/

Life Insurance (IRDAI + LIC):
  Non-early claims: settle within 30 days (IRDAI mandate)
  Early claims (<3 yrs): max 90 days
  Docs: Form A (3783), death cert, original policy, claimant ID, NEFT mandate
  LIC: any branch, Download Form 3783
    Link: https://licindia.in/claims-services
  Contact: 022-68276827 | claimshelpdesk@licindia.in
  IRDAI Grievance: https://igms.irda.gov.in/
  Toll-free: 155255

EPF/PF (EPFO):
  Form 10D: monthly pension (widow/dependents)
  Form 20: lump-sum PF withdrawal
  Form 5IF: EDLI insurance claim (up to ₹7 lakh)
  Submit via employer HR or EPFO portal (if UAN active)
  Docs: death cert, UAN, nominee/heir details, bank proof
  Processing: 20 working days
  Portal: https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in/
  Helpline: 1800-118-005
  Grievance: https://epfigms.gov.in/

Demat/Stocks (SEBI Guidelines):
  With nomination: 7-day settlement
  Without: transmission form + death cert + succession cert + indemnity bond
  Below ₹5L market value: indemnity bond only
  Contact broker/DP directly for transmission form
  NSDL: https://www.nsdl.co.in/transmission.php
  CDSL: https://www.cdslindia.com/
  SEBI: https://www.sebi.gov.in/

Mutual Funds:
  With nominee: Submit death cert + nominee KYC + transmission form to AMC/registrar
  Without nominee: Legal heir certificate + consent letter from all heirs
  CAMS: https://www.camsonline.com/ (for most AMCs)
  KFintech: https://www.kfintech.com/ (for remaining AMCs)
  Process: 7-15 working days
  Important: Units are transmitted, not redeemed. Tax applies only on future redemption.

PPF (Public Provident Fund):
  Nominee receives balance + interest up to date of death
  If no nominee: Legal heir with succession certificate
  Submit at the Post Office or bank where PPF is held
  Docs: death cert, nominee ID, PPF passbook, application form
  Process: 15-30 working days
  Link: https://www.indiapost.gov.in/

NPS (National Pension System):
  Pre-retirement death: Entire corpus to nominee as lump sum
  Post-retirement death: Depends on annuity type chosen
  Submit claim through POP (Point of Presence)
  Link: https://www.npscra.nsdl.co.in/

UPI Wallets:
  No formal nomination system in India for wallets
  Contact support with death cert + claimant ID
  PhonePe: support.phonepe.com → deceased account report
    Link: https://support.phonepe.com/
  Google Pay: Google account support
    Link: https://support.google.com/googlepay/
  Paytm: care@paytm.com subject "Deceased Account – [Phone]"
    Link: https://paytm.com/care/
  Important: Transfer any wallet balance BEFORE deactivating mobile SIM

Email Accounts:
  Gmail: Inactive Account Manager or deceased user request
    Link: https://support.google.com/accounts/troubleshooter/6357590
  No account access given; only closure/data download possible
  Google Takeout (for data before closure): https://takeout.google.com/
  Outlook/Microsoft: deceased user request form
    Link: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/concern/deceased
  Yahoo: Send death cert + proof to Yahoo support
  Processing: 2-4 weeks

Social Media:
  Facebook: memorialization or removal request
    Memorialization: https://www.facebook.com/help/1506822589577997
    Removal: https://www.facebook.com/help/228813257197480
  Instagram: removal only (no memorialization for Instagram)
    Link: https://help.instagram.com/264154560391256
  Twitter/X: deactivation request
    Link: https://help.twitter.com/forms/privacy
  LinkedIn: memorialization request
    Link: https://www.linkedin.com/help/linkedin/answer/a1342062
  WhatsApp: unlink SIM; auto-deletes after 120 days of inactivity
  YouTube: Covered under Google Account closure

Subscriptions and Recurring Payments:
  List from bank statements and UPI AutoPay
  Contact each service's support
  Most will cancel + provide pro-rata refund with death cert
  Cancel UPI AutoPay via bank's UPI app
  Common services to check:
    - Netflix, Amazon Prime, Disney+, Spotify
    - Newspapers/magazines subscriptions
    - Cloud storage (Google One, iCloud, OneDrive)
    - Domain names, web hosting
    - Gym memberships
    - OTT platforms

Vehicle Registration (RTO):
  Submit Form 31 to RTO for transfer
  Docs: death cert, legal heir cert, RC book, insurance, PUC
  If vehicle loan exists: NOC from bank after loan settlement
  Link: https://parivahan.gov.in/
  Process: 15-30 working days

EPF / Provident Fund Claims After Death (India — EPFO Guidelines):

OVERVIEW:
When an EPF member passes away, the nominee or legal heir can claim the accumulated PF balance, pension (EPS), and insurance (EDLI). Three separate forms cover these benefits. The total claim can be substantial as it includes employer + employee contributions + interest.

THREE TYPES OF CLAIMS:

1. EPF WITHDRAWAL (Form 20):
- Lump-sum withdrawal of full PF balance
- Available to: Nominee (if registered) or legal heir
- Amount: Full PF accumulation (employee + employer share + interest)

2. PENSION CLAIM (Form 10D):
- Monthly pension for widow/widower and children
- Available to: Spouse of deceased member
- EPS pension: Based on years of service and average salary
- Children's pension: Until age 25 (two children maximum)
- Orphan pension: If both parents deceased

3. EDLI INSURANCE (Form 5IF):
- Employees' Deposit Linked Insurance Scheme
- Lump-sum insurance payout
- Maximum benefit: Up to ₹7 lakh (based on last 12 months average balance)
- Available to: Nominee or family members

STEP 1: Gather Information
- Deceased's UAN (Universal Account Number) — check salary slips or EPFO portal
- Member ID / PF number
- Employer details (last employer)
- Aadhaar and bank details linked to UAN

STEP 2: Contact Employer HR
- Inform the employer about the death
- Request employer to:
  - Attest the claim forms
  - Provide employment details and wage certificate
  - Submit forms to EPFO (employer attestation is mandatory for offline claims)

STEP 3: Prepare Documents
REQUIRED DOCUMENTS:
  - Death certificate (certified copy)
  - Relationship proof with deceased (marriage certificate, ration card, family ID)
  - Nominee's/claimant's Aadhaar card
  - Nominee's/claimant's PAN card
  - Nominee's/claimant's bank account details (cancelled cheque / passbook copy)
  - UAN card or member passbook printout
  - Form 20 (PF withdrawal) — duly filled and attested by employer
  - Form 10D (pension) — if claiming EPS pension
  - Form 5IF (EDLI insurance) — for insurance benefit
  - Succession certificate / Legal heir certificate (if no nominee registered)

STEP 4: Submit Claims
OPTION A — Online (if UAN is Aadhaar-linked):
  - Login to EPFO Member Portal: https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in/
  - Navigate to "Online Services" > "Claim (Form-31, 19, 10D, 10C)"
  - Select appropriate claim type
  - Upload required documents
  - Submit electronically

OPTION B — Offline:
  - Download forms from https://www.epfindia.gov.in/site_en/Forms.php
  - Fill and get attested by employer
  - Submit at the regional EPFO office with jurisdiction over the member's account
  - Get acknowledgment receipt

STEP 5: Processing
- Online claims: 10-20 working days
- Offline claims: 20-30 working days
- If KYC is incomplete or mismatch: EPFO will return the form for correction

STEP 6: Receive Payment
- PF balance credited to claimant's bank account via NEFT
- EPS pension credited monthly
- EDLI amount credited as lump sum

SPECIAL CASES:
- If employer is closed/non-responsive: Submit directly to EPFO with an affidavit explaining the situation
- If UAN is not activated: Visit EPFO office with member details for manual processing
- Multiple PF accounts: Each account needs separate claim
- International workers: Special provisions under Social Security Agreements

EPFO CONTACT:
- Portal: https://www.epfindia.gov.in/
- Member Portal: https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in/
- Helpline: 1800-118-005 (toll-free)
- Grievance: https://epfigms.gov.in/
- Email: Check regional office contacts on EPFO website

ESCALATION:
- If claim is delayed beyond 30 days: File grievance on EPF iGMS portal
  Link: https://epfigms.gov.in/
- If rejected: Appeal to Regional PF Commissioner
- Further appeal: EPF Appellate Tribunal
- RTI: File RTI application to track status

TAX IMPLICATIONS:
- EPF withdrawal after 5 years of service: TAX-FREE
- EPF withdrawal before 5 years: Taxable as income
- EPS pension: Taxable as salary income
- EDLI: TAX-FREE

TIMELINE SUMMARY:
- Gather documents: 1-2 weeks
- Employer attestation: 1 week
- EPFO processing: 2-4 weeks
- Total expected: 4-8 weeks

Life Insurance Claims After Death (India — IRDAI Guidelines):

OVERVIEW:
Life insurance is one of the most important financial benefits a family can claim after a death. IRDAI mandates that insurers settle non-early death claims within 30 days of receiving all documents. Early death claims (within 3 years of policy) may take up to 90 days for investigation.

TYPES OF DEATH CLAIMS:
1. Non-Early Claim: Death occurs after 3 years of policy commencement. Straightforward settlement.
2. Early Claim: Death occurs within 3 years. Insurer may investigate cause of death.
3. Accidental Death Claim: If policy has accidental death benefit rider, additional payout applies.

STEP 1: Locate the Policy Document
- Check physical files, email (search "policy", "premium", "LIC", "insurance"), bank statements for premium debits
- If policy document is lost: Contact insurer for duplicate. Online policies are accessible via insurer's portal.

STEP 2: Intimate the Insurance Company
- Call the insurer's claims helpdesk immediately
- Register an intimation online or at the nearest branch
- Note down the claim reference number

STEP 3: Collect Required Documents
FOR NOMINEE:
  - Claim Form (Form A / specific insurer form)
  - Death certificate (original or certified copy)
  - Original policy document (or indemnity bond if lost)
  - Nominee's identity proof (Aadhaar + PAN)
  - Nominee's photo
  - NEFT mandate form / cancelled cheque of nominee's bank account
  - Hospital records / treatment summary (if death due to illness)
  - FIR + Post-mortem report (if accidental/unnatural death)
  - Employer certificate (if group insurance)

IF NO NOMINEE:
  - Legal Heir Certificate or Succession Certificate
  - All documents listed above
  - Affidavit of all legal heirs with consent for one person to receive the claim

STEP 4: Submit Documents
- Submit at the nearest branch or through the insurer's online portal
- Get acknowledgment receipt with claim number and expected timeline

STEP 5: Claim Processing
- Non-early claims: 30 days from document submission
- Early claims: Up to 90 days (investigation may be conducted)
- If insurer rejects or delays: Written reason must be provided

STEP 6: Receive Payout
- Payment via NEFT to nominee's/legal heir's bank account
- Tax: Life insurance death claim payout is TAX-FREE under Section 10(10D) of Income Tax Act

LIC (Life Insurance Corporation) SPECIFIC:
- Form: Claim Form A (Form 3783)
  Download: https://licindia.in/claims-services
- Submit at any LIC branch (not limited to servicing branch)
- LIC Customer Care: 022-68276827
- Email: claimshelpdesk@licindia.in
- Online claim status: https://licindia.in/claim-status
- Link: https://licindia.in/

PRIVATE INSURERS:
- HDFC Life: 1860-267-9999 | https://www.hdfclife.com/claims
- ICICI Prudential: 1860-266-7766 | https://www.iciciprulife.com/claims
- SBI Life: 1800-267-9090 | https://www.sbilife.co.in/en/claims
- Max Life: 1860-120-5577 | https://www.maxlifeinsurance.com/claims
- Bajaj Allianz: 1800-209-7272 | https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/claims

HEALTH INSURANCE:
- If the deceased had a health insurance policy and incurred medical expenses before death:
  - File a health insurance claim for hospitalization costs
  - Submit: Hospital bills, discharge summary, death certificate, policy document
  - Timeline: 30 days as per IRDAI

ESCALATION:
- If claim is rejected: Request written reason. Appeal within 30 days.
- If delayed beyond timelines: Complain to IRDAI
  - IRDAI Grievance: igms.irda.gov.in
  - IRDAI Toll-free: 155255
  - Email: complaints@irdai.gov.in
- Insurance Ombudsman: For claims up to ₹30 lakh
  Link: https://www.cioins.co.in/

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- Do NOT delay intimation. Inform the insurer within 24-48 hours if possible.
- Keep copies of all submitted documents.
- If multiple policies exist, file separate claims for each.
- Group insurance through employer: Contact HR department.
- Ensure NEFT details are correct to avoid payment delays.

Loans and Liabilities Settlement After Death (India):

OVERVIEW:
Debts and liabilities do not automatically disappear when a person dies. They must be settled from the deceased's estate. However, legal heirs are NOT personally liable for debts beyond the value of inherited assets (unless they are co-borrowers or guarantors).

KEY PRINCIPLE:
- Debts are settled from the deceased's estate (assets)
- Legal heirs are NOT personally liable (unless co-borrower/guarantor)
- Secured loans: Lender can seize the pledged asset
- Unsecured loans: Recovered from estate; cannot pursue heirs personally

TYPE 1: HOME LOAN / MORTGAGE

SCENARIO A: With Co-borrower
- Co-borrower continues to pay EMIs
- Transfer loan to co-borrower's name
- Submit death certificate to bank
- If co-borrower cannot pay: Sell property, settle loan, distribute remainder

SCENARIO B: Without Co-borrower — Insurance Exists
- Most home loans have credit life insurance
- File claim with insurance company
- Insurance settles the outstanding loan amount
- Property becomes debt-free for legal heirs
- Check if the deceased had PMJJBY (₹2 lakh cover) or bank's group insurance

SCENARIO C: Without Co-borrower — No Insurance
STEP 1: Inform the bank about the death
STEP 2: Submit death certificate and legal heir documents
STEP 3: Options:
  a) Legal heir continues EMI payments and retains property
  b) Bank sells property (with heir's consent), settles loan, returns surplus
  c) If property value < loan amount: Bank writes off the shortfall (heirs not liable for deficit)
STEP 4: Transfer property title to heir's name after loan settlement

REQUIRED DOCUMENTS:
- Death certificate
- Legal Heir Certificate / Succession Certificate
- Original loan agreement
- Property documents
- Insurance policy (if applicable)
- KYC of claimant/heir

TYPE 2: PERSONAL LOAN

- Personal loans are UNSECURED — no collateral
- If deceased had loan insurance: Insurance settles the loan
- If no insurance: Loan is recovered from the estate
- If no assets in estate: Bank writes off the loan
- Legal heirs CANNOT be forced to pay from their personal funds
- Exception: If heir is a co-borrower or guarantor, they are liable

IMPORTANT: If bank/recovery agents harass heirs for personal loan repayment:
- This is ILLEGAL (as per RBI Fair Practice Code)
- File complaint with Banking Ombudsman
  Link: https://cms.rbi.org.in/
- Report to police if harassment continues

TYPE 3: VEHICLE LOAN

- Vehicle loan is secured against the vehicle
- If loan insurance exists: Insurance settles remaining amount
- If no insurance:
  a) Heir continues EMIs and retains vehicle (transfer to heir's name)
  b) Surrender vehicle to lender
  c) Sell vehicle, settle loan, keep remainder
- Vehicle RC transfer also required (see RTO process)
- RTO: Submit death certificate + legal heir certificate + Form 31

TYPE 4: CREDIT CARD DEBT

- Credit card debt is UNSECURED
- Card issuer will attempt recovery from estate
- If no assets: Debt is written off
- Heirs are NOT liable unless they are supplementary card holders
- Inform the bank to cancel the credit card immediately
- Outstanding auto-debits/subscriptions will stop once card is cancelled

TYPE 5: GOLD LOAN

- Gold loan is secured against pledged gold
- Options:
  a) Pay outstanding amount and retrieve gold
  b) If unable to pay: Bank auctions the gold and settles the loan
  c) Surplus from auction returned to heir
- Submit death certificate and legal heir documents

TYPE 6: EDUCATION LOAN

- If deceased is the student (borrower): Co-borrower/guarantor is liable
- If deceased is the parent (co-borrower): Student continues payments
- Insurance (if any): Settles the loan
- Moratorium: Banks may grant temporary moratorium for hardship

TYPE 7: BUSINESS LOANS

- Sole proprietorship: Personal liability — settled from estate
- Partnership firm: Other partners may be jointly liable
- Private Limited Company: Company's liability, not personal
- Directors: Only liable if they gave personal guarantees

GENERAL STEPS FOR ALL LOANS:

STEP 1: Inform all lenders about the death (within 1-2 weeks)
STEP 2: Submit death certificate to each lender
STEP 3: Request outstanding balance statement
STEP 4: Check for loan insurance / credit life insurance
STEP 5: If insured: File insurance claim to settle the loan
STEP 6: If uninsured: Negotiate with lender for settlement/closure
STEP 7: Obtain No Dues Certificate (NDC) after settlement
STEP 8: Keep NDC safely — it protects heirs from future claims

CREDIT REPORT:
- CIBIL record of the deceased should be updated
- Submit death certificate to CIBIL/TransUnion
  Link: https://www.cibil.com/
- Prevents identity theft and fraudulent applications

GOVERNMENT SCHEMES:
- PMJJBY (Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana): ₹2 lakh life cover, ₹436/year premium
  - If deceased was enrolled: File claim with bank
  - Link: https://www.jansuraksha.gov.in/
- PMSBY (Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana): ₹2 lakh accidental death cover
  - File claim at the bank where premium was debited
  - Link: https://www.jansuraksha.gov.in/

ESCALATION:
- Bank harassment: Banking Ombudsman (https://cms.rbi.org.in/)
- NBFC harassment: RBI NBFC complaint
- Recovery agent misconduct: Police complaint + RBI complaint
- Legal recourse: Consumer Court for unfair bank practices

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- Never sign any document acknowledging personal liability for the deceased's debts
- Recovery agents cannot force entry, seize property without court order, or harass
- Joint account debits after death: Inform bank immediately to freeze the deceased's share
- Get legal advice if debt amounts are large or recovery pressure is intense

Nominee vs Legal Heir — Understanding the Difference (India):

OVERVIEW:
One of the most common confusions in post-death processes is the difference between a nominee and a legal heir. Understanding this distinction is critical for claiming assets effectively.

NOMINEE:
- A nominee is a person designated by the account/policy holder to receive the proceeds upon death.
- The nominee acts as a CUSTODIAN or TRUSTEE, not necessarily the legal owner.
- Supreme Court ruling (Sarbati Devi vs Usha Devi, 1984): A nominee holds the assets in trust for the legal heirs unless the nominee IS the legal heir.
- Nomination simplifies the transfer process but does NOT override a will or succession law.
- Banks, insurance companies, and mutual funds are legally required to pay the nominee.

LEGAL HEIR:
- Legal heirs are determined by succession law (Hindu Succession Act, Indian Succession Act, Muslim Personal Law, etc.).
- They are the rightful owners of the deceased's estate.
- If there is a WILL: The beneficiaries named in the will are the legal heirs.
- If there is NO WILL (intestate succession): Legal heirs are determined by applicable personal law.

HINDU SUCCESSION ACT — CLASS I HEIRS:
- Son, Daughter, Widow/Widower
- Mother
- Son of a pre-deceased son
- Daughter of a pre-deceased son
- Son of a pre-deceased daughter
- Daughter of a pre-deceased daughter
- Widow of a pre-deceased son
All Class I heirs have EQUAL share in the property.

LEGAL HEIR CERTIFICATE:
STEP 1: Apply at Tehsildar / Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) office
STEP 2: Submit:
  - Death certificate
  - Applicant's identity proof (Aadhaar)
  - Address proof
  - Relationship proof (ration card, family ID)
  - Affidavit sworn before a Notary/Magistrate listing all legal heirs
  - Two witnesses with their ID proofs
STEP 3: Verification by Revenue official (may include field inquiry)
STEP 4: Certificate issued within 15-30 days
- Cost: ₹50-200 (varies by state)
- Link (varies by state): Check e-District portal of your state

SUCCESSION CERTIFICATE:
- Issued by Civil Court under Indian Succession Act, Section 372
- Required for: Movable property (bank accounts, shares, FDs) when balance is high and no nominee exists
- More legally robust than Legal Heir Certificate

STEP 1: File petition in the Civil Court having jurisdiction over the deceased's last residence
STEP 2: Required documents:
  - Death certificate
  - List of assets/debts
  - Relationship proof
  - Details of all legal heirs
  - Affidavit
  - Court fee (generally 2-3% of asset value, varies by state)
STEP 3: Court publishes notice in newspaper (45 days waiting period for objections)
STEP 4: If no objections, court issues Succession Certificate
- Timeline: 2-6 months
- Cost: Court fees + lawyer fees + newspaper publication charges

WHEN IS WHAT NEEDED:
| Scenario | Document Needed |
|---|---|
| Bank account ≤ ₹5L, no nominee | Letter of Indemnity (no succession cert needed per RBI) |
| Bank account > ₹5L, no nominee | Legal Heir Certificate or Succession Certificate |
| Insurance claim, nominee exists | Nominee's claim form only |
| Insurance claim, no nominee | Legal Heir Certificate + consent of all heirs |
| Property transfer | Succession Certificate or Probate of Will |
| EPF claim, nominee exists | Form 20 with nominee details |
| EPF claim, no nominee | Legal Heir Certificate |
| Shares/Demat, no nominee | Succession Certificate (mandatory for SEBI-regulated assets) |

WILL AND PROBATE:
- If the deceased left a valid will: Probate may be required in some states (mandatory in Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai)
- Probate: Court-certified validation of a will
- Without probate (where not mandatory): Will + death certificate may suffice for many purposes
- Registered Will vs Unregistered Will: Registration is NOT mandatory but registered wills have stronger legal standing

IMPORTANT TIPS:
- Get Legal Heir Certificate FIRST — it's faster and cheaper than Succession Certificate
- Many institutions accept Legal Heir Certificate for amounts up to ₹5-10 lakh
- For larger amounts or disputed cases, Succession Certificate is more reliable
- If there's a dispute among heirs: Legal consultation recommended
- Keep multiple certified copies of whatever certificate you obtain

Pension Claims After Death (India):

OVERVIEW:
When a pensioner passes away, the spouse and dependents may be eligible for family pension. The process varies based on whether the deceased was a government employee, private sector employee (EPS/NPS), or a military/defense personnel.

TYPE 1: GOVERNMENT PENSION (Central/State Government Employees)

FAMILY PENSION ELIGIBILITY:
- Spouse (widow/widower) — until death or remarriage
- Unmarried/divorced/widowed daughters — until marriage/remarriage or employment
- Sons — until age 25 or employment
- Disabled children — for lifetime

STEP 1: Inform the Pension Disbursing Authority
  - For Central Govt: Treasury Office / Bank branch where pension is credited
  - For State Govt: State Treasury / Directorate of Pensions

STEP 2: Submit Required Documents
  - Death certificate (certified copy)
  - PPO (Pension Payment Order) number
  - Family pension application form
  - Spouse/dependent's identity proof (Aadhaar + PAN)
  - Spouse/dependent's bank account details
  - Marriage certificate (for spouse)
  - Birth certificate of dependent children (if applicable)
  - Joint photo of deceased and spouse (if available)
  - Life certificate (Jeevan Pramaan) of the claimant

STEP 3: Apply for Family Pension
  - Central Govt: Through CPAO (Central Pension Accounting Office)
    Link: https://cpao.nic.in/
  - Defence: Through PCDA (Principal Controller of Defence Accounts)
    Link: https://pcdapension.nic.in/
  - State Govt: Through respective State Pension Department
  - CCS (Pension) Rules, 2021 applicable for Central Govt
    Link: https://doppw.gov.in/

STEP 4: Processing
  - Family pension should commence from the day after the pensioner's death
  - Enhanced family pension: 50% of last pay for first 7 years or up to age 67, whichever is earlier
  - Normal family pension: 30% of last pay thereafter
  - Timeline: 1-3 months for processing

STEP 5: Arrears and Gratuity
  - Any pending pension arrears will be paid to the family
  - Death Gratuity: Payable if death occurs while in service (up to 16.5 times of pay, max ₹20 lakh)

TYPE 2: EPS PENSION (Employees' Pension Scheme — Private Sector)

ELIGIBILITY:
- Spouse and up to 2 children of deceased EPF member
- Member must have completed at least 10 years of service

FAMILY PENSION:
- Widow pension: Based on service length and average salary
- Children's pension: Additional 25% of widow pension per child (max 2)
- Orphan pension: 75% of widow pension per child

STEP 1: Submit Form 10D to EPFO
  - Download: https://www.epfindia.gov.in/site_en/Forms.php
STEP 2: Required Documents:
  - Form 10D (duly filled)
  - Death certificate
  - Marriage certificate
  - Spouse's Aadhaar and bank details
  - UAN of deceased member
  - Employer attestation (if available)
STEP 3: Submit to regional EPFO office or online via member portal
STEP 4: Processing: 20-30 working days
  Link: https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in/

TYPE 3: NPS (National Pension System)

IF SUBSCRIBER DIES BEFORE RETIREMENT:
- Entire accumulated corpus paid to nominee/legal heir as lump sum
- No mandatory annuity purchase required

IF SUBSCRIBER DIES AFTER RETIREMENT:
- Depends on annuity type chosen:
  - If "Return of Purchase Price" annuity: Lump sum returned to nominee
  - If "Joint Life" annuity: Pension continues to spouse

STEP 1: Nominee/heir submits claim to NPS Point of Presence (POP)
  - Or through employer (for government NPS subscribers)
STEP 2: Documents:
  - Death certificate
  - PRAN (Permanent Retirement Account Number) card
  - Nominee's/heir's KYC
  - Bank account details
STEP 3: CRA (Central Recordkeeping Agency) processes the claim
  Link: https://www.npscra.nsdl.co.in/
  NSDL CRA: https://cra-nsdl.com/
STEP 4: Processing: 15-30 working days

TYPE 4: MILITARY / DEFENCE PENSION

- Contact Unit Record Office or Zila Sainik Board
- Family pension under CCS (Pension) Rules or Defence Pension Rules
- Additional benefits: Canteen facilities, medical coverage (ECHS)
- ECHS: https://echs.gov.in/
- SPARSH Portal: https://sparsh.defencepension.gov.in/

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- Family pension is a RIGHT, not a favor. Do not accept delays.
- If pension is credited to joint bank account with survivor mode: Pension continues automatically until the bank is informed about the death. Inform promptly to avoid recovery.
- Jeevan Pramaan (Digital Life Certificate) can be submitted online: https://jeevanpramaan.gov.in/
- Commuted portion of pension stands restored after 15 years from commutation.

ESCALATION:
- Central Govt: Department of Pension & Pensioners' Welfare
  Helpline: 1800-11-1960
  Link: https://doppw.gov.in/
- Pensioners' Grievance: https://pgportal.gov.in/
- If bank delays family pension payment: Approach bank nodal officer or Banking Ombudsman

Property Transfer After Death (India):

OVERVIEW:
Transferring property (immovable assets) after death is one of the most complex post-death processes. It involves mutation of land/property records, which is handled by the Revenue Department or Municipal Corporation.

TYPES OF PROPERTY:
1. Self-acquired property: Governed by the will (if exists) or succession laws
2. Ancestral property: Governed by Hindu Succession Act or applicable personal law
3. Joint property: Survivorship rights may apply (depends on form of ownership)

SCENARIO A: With a Valid Will
STEP 1: Obtain Probate of Will (mandatory in Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai; optional elsewhere)
  - File petition in District Court
  - Submit: Original will, death certificate, list of heirs, court fee
  - Timeline: 3-12 months
STEP 2: Apply for Mutation at local Revenue/Municipal office
STEP 3: Submit documents (see checklist below)
STEP 4: Mutation recorded in property records
- Link: State Revenue Department portal

SCENARIO B: Without a Will (Intestate Succession)
STEP 1: Obtain Legal Heir Certificate or Succession Certificate
STEP 2: If multiple heirs exist and one wants sole ownership: Execute a Release Deed or Family Settlement Deed
STEP 3: Apply for Mutation at local Revenue/Municipal office
STEP 4: Submit documents (see checklist below)
STEP 5: Mutation recorded

STEP-BY-STEP MUTATION PROCESS:
STEP 1: Collect all property documents
  - Sale deed / title deed
  - Previous mutation records
  - Property tax receipts
  - Encumbrance certificate (from Sub-Registrar office)

STEP 2: Apply for Mutation
  - Urban property: Apply at Municipal Corporation / Nagar Palika
  - Rural property: Apply at Tehsildar / Revenue office
  - Online portals available in many states:
    - Maharashtra: https://mahabhulekh.maharashtra.gov.in/
    - Karnataka: https://landrecords.karnataka.gov.in/
    - Uttar Pradesh: https://upbhulekh.gov.in/
    - Tamil Nadu: https://eservices.tn.gov.in/

STEP 3: Submit Required Documents
  - Application form for mutation
  - Death certificate (certified copy)
  - Legal Heir Certificate / Succession Certificate / Probate
  - Original sale deed / title deed (for verification)
  - Property tax paid receipts (latest)
  - Encumbrance certificate (last 13-30 years)
  - NOC from other legal heirs (if applicable)
  - Release Deed / Family Settlement (if applicable)
  - Identity proof of applicant (Aadhaar + PAN)
  - Two passport-size photos

STEP 4: Verification
  - Revenue inspector may conduct field inspection
  - Notice may be published for objections (15-30 days)
  - Hearing scheduled if objections are raised

STEP 5: Mutation Order
  - Revenue officer passes mutation order
  - Property records updated in the name of legal heir(s)
  - Get certified copy of mutation record

ADDITIONAL STEPS:
- Update property tax records at Municipal Corporation
- Update electricity/water connection names
- Update Aadhaar address (if moving into property)
- Register the property transfer at Sub-Registrar office (stamp duty may apply)

STAMP DUTY AND REGISTRATION:
- Mutation by succession: Reduced stamp duty in most states
- Some states exempt stamp duty for transfers to spouse/children by succession
- Registration at Sub-Registrar: Typically needed for updating the sale deed
- Check state-specific stamp duty rates

HOUSING SOCIETY (Apartments):
- Inform the Housing Society in writing with death certificate
- Apply for transfer of share certificate
- Submit: Death certificate, Legal Heir Certificate, NOC from other heirs
- Society may charge a nominal transfer fee
- Update maintenance billing name

AGRICULTURAL LAND:
- Special rules apply in many states
- Some states restrict transfer to non-agriculturists
- Revenue records (7/12 extract in Maharashtra, Khata in Karnataka) must be updated
- Approach Tehsildar office

DISPUTES AND LITIGATION:
- If heirs disagree: Mediation recommended first
- If mediation fails: Civil suit for partition
- If illegal possession by others: File suit for possession in Civil Court
- Legal aid available for economically weaker sections

TIMELINE SUMMARY:
- Document collection: 2-4 weeks
- Legal Heir / Succession Certificate: 2 weeks to 6 months
- Mutation application & processing: 1-3 months
- Total: 3-9 months (varies significantly by state and complexity)

IMPORTANT NOTE:
- Property mutation does NOT transfer ownership by itself. It is an update of revenue records.
- Legal ownership transfer happens through Will/Succession, not mutation.
- Always consult a local property lawyer for specific cases.

Tax-Related Steps After Death (India — Income Tax):

OVERVIEW:
The deceased's tax obligations do not end with death. The legal representative (legal heir or executor of will) is responsible for filing the deceased's final income tax return and handling any tax-related matters.

WHO IS RESPONSIBLE:
- Legal Representative: The person who manages the deceased's estate
- As per Section 159 of Income Tax Act: The legal representative is liable to pay taxes from the estate
- Personal liability of legal representative is limited to the extent of assets received

STEP 1: Register as Legal Representative on Income Tax Portal
- Visit: https://www.incometax.gov.in/
- Login using the legal representative's own PAN
- Navigate to: "Authorize to Act as Representative Assessee" (under "My Account" or "Compliance")
- File Form 2 to register as legal representative of the deceased
- Submit: Death certificate, Legal Heir Certificate, PAN of deceased
- Once approved, you can file returns on behalf of the deceased

STEP 2: File Final Income Tax Return
- File ITR for the period from April 1st to date of death
- Income from all sources until date of death must be declared:
  - Salary received
  - Interest earned on FDs, savings accounts
  - Rental income
  - Capital gains (if any investments were sold)
  - Business income (if applicable)
- Due date: Same as regular ITR filing deadline (July 31 for individuals)
- If TDS was deducted: Claim refund if applicable

STEP 3: Income After Date of Death
- Income earned AFTER the date of death belongs to the estate or legal heirs
- If assets are not yet distributed: File return as "Estate of [Deceased Name]" through the legal representative
- Once assets are distributed: Each heir files their own return including inherited income

STEP 4: Pending Tax Demands
- Check the income tax portal for any pending demands against the deceased
- If legitimate: Pay from the estate
- If incorrect: Respond/appeal through the portal as legal representative
- Outstanding refunds: Will be credited to the bank account linked to deceased's PAN

STEP 5: PAN Status Update
- After all tax matters are settled, the deceased's PAN should be marked as "inactive"
- Submit request to Jurisdictional Assessing Officer (JAO)
- This prevents misuse of the PAN

CAPITAL GAINS ON INHERITED ASSETS:
- Inheritance itself is NOT taxable (there is no inheritance tax in India)
- However, when the heir SELLS an inherited asset, capital gains tax applies
- Cost of acquisition: The cost at which the deceased originally acquired the asset
- Period of holding: Includes the deceased's period of holding
- For property: Indexation benefit available for long-term capital gains

SPECIFIC TAX SCENARIOS:

Interest on FDs/Bank Accounts:
- Interest accrued until date of death: Include in deceased's final return
- Interest accrued after date of death: Include in heir's return (or estate return)
- TDS certificates (Form 16A) can be obtained from the bank

Mutual Funds / Shares:
- No tax on transmission to nominee/heir
- Tax arises only when the heir sells/redeems the units
- Capital gains calculated from original purchase date and cost

Life Insurance Proceeds:
- EXEMPT from tax under Section 10(10D) — no tax filing required for this amount

EPF/PPF Proceeds:
- EPF received by nominee/heir: Generally tax-free (if member had 5+ years of service)
- PPF maturity: Fully tax-exempt

Pension:
- Family pension received by spouse/heir: TAXABLE as "Income from other sources"
- Standard deduction of ₹15,000 or 1/3rd of pension (whichever is lower) is available
- Report under ITR-1 or ITR-2

STEP 6: Clear All Pending Obligations
- File any pending returns for previous years
- Settle any outstanding self-assessment tax
- Respond to any notices from the Income Tax Department
- Maintain records for at least 7 years

REQUIRED DOCUMENTS FOR TAX FILING:
- Deceased's PAN card
- Death certificate
- Legal Heir Certificate
- Form 16 / Form 16A (salary/TDS certificates)
- Bank statements (for interest income)
- Investment proofs (mutual funds, shares, FDs)
- Property documents (if rental income was earned)
- Previous year's ITR (for reference)

IMPORTANT LINKS:
- Income Tax Portal: https://www.incometax.gov.in/
- e-Filing Help: https://www.incometax.gov.in/iec/foportal/help
- PAN services: https://www.onlineservices.nsdl.com/paam/endUserRegisterContact.html
- TDS certificates: Ask bank/employer or check Form 26AS on IT portal

ESCALATION:
- For errors in filing: Approach local Income Tax office (Jurisdictional AO)
- For refund delays: File grievance at https://www.incometax.gov.in/iec/foportal/ (Grievance tab)
- Chartered Accountant: Recommended for complex tax situations

TIMELINE:
- Register as legal representative: 1-2 weeks
- File final return: Before July 31 of the assessment year (or within time limit for belated return)
- Process refunds: 3-6 months after filing
- Settle demands: Within 30 days of receiving notice

Utility Account Closure / Transfer After Death (India):

OVERVIEW:
Utility accounts (electricity, water, gas, telephone, internet) in the deceased's name should be either transferred to a family member or closed to avoid accumulating bills and penalties.

ELECTRICITY CONNECTION:

STEP 1: Gather documents
  - Death certificate
  - Last paid electricity bill
  - Legal Heir Certificate or relationship proof
  - Identity proof of new connection holder
  - Property ownership/occupancy proof

STEP 2: Visit local electricity office or apply online
  - BSES (Delhi): https://www.bsesdelhi.com/
  - TATA Power (Mumbai): https://www.tatapower.com/
  - BESCOM (Bangalore): https://bescom.karnataka.gov.in/
  - TNEB (Tamil Nadu): https://www.tnebnet.org/
  - MSEDCL (Maharashtra): https://www.mahadiscom.in/

STEP 3: Submit name transfer application with documents
STEP 4: Pay any outstanding dues
STEP 5: New connection issued in heir's name (existing meter retained)
- Timeline: 7-15 working days
- If closing: Request disconnection and final bill settlement

WATER CONNECTION:

STEP 1: Visit local Municipal Corporation water department
STEP 2: Submit:
  - Death certificate
  - Identity proof of applicant
  - Property proof
  - Transfer application form
STEP 3: Pay pending water charges
STEP 4: Connection transferred or new connection issued
- Timeline: 7-21 working days

GAS CONNECTION (LPG):

STEP 1: Visit the nearest distributor (Indane/HP/Bharat Gas)
STEP 2: Submit:
  - Death certificate
  - Heir's Aadhaar and PAN
  - Address proof
  - Transfer application (Distributor Form 17)
  - Ration card showing heir as family member
STEP 3: Distributor processes transfer
STEP 4: Subsidy account linked to heir's bank (for DBTL)
- Timeline: 7-15 working days
- Indane: https://www.iocl.com/indane
- HP Gas: https://www.hindustanpetroleum.com/hpgas
- Bharat Gas: https://www.bharatgas.com/

PIPED NATURAL GAS (PNG):
- Contact the gas distribution company
  - IGL (Delhi): https://www.iglonline.net/
  - MGL (Mumbai): https://www.mahanagargas.com/
  - GAIL Gas: https://gailgas.com/
- Submit death certificate + heir's KYC + property proof
- Timeline: 7-15 working days

TELEPHONE / LANDLINE:

STEP 1: Visit BSNL/MTNL/Airtel/Jio office
STEP 2: Submit:
  - Death certificate
  - Identity proof of heir
  - Request for name transfer or disconnection
STEP 3: Settle outstanding bills
STEP 4: Transfer processed or line disconnected
- BSNL: https://portal.bsnl.in/
- Timeline: 7-15 working days
- If disconnecting: Return any equipment (modem, set-top box)

INTERNET / BROADBAND:

STEP 1: Contact the ISP (Internet Service Provider)
  - Airtel: https://www.airtel.in/
  - Jio Fiber: https://www.jio.com/fiber
  - ACT Fibernet: https://www.actcorp.in/
  - BSNL: https://portal.bsnl.in/
STEP 2: Request closure or name transfer
STEP 3: Submit death certificate + KYC
STEP 4: Return any rented equipment (router, ONT)
STEP 5: Settle final bill and get security deposit refund
- Timeline: 7-15 working days

MOBILE PHONE CONNECTION:

STEP 1: Visit the service provider's store
STEP 2: Options:
  a) Transfer to heir: Submit death certificate + heir's Aadhaar + application
  b) Close permanently: Submit death certificate + surrender form
STEP 3: Retrieve important data (contacts, photos) before closure
STEP 4: If postpaid: Settle outstanding bills
STEP 5: SIM deactivated or ported
- Important: If the mobile number is linked to bank accounts/UPI, update the registered mobile number with those institutions FIRST before closing the SIM.

CABLE TV / DTH:

STEP 1: Contact the DTH provider
  - Tata Play: https://www.tataplay.com/
  - Airtel Digital TV: https://www.airtel.in/digital-tv
  - Dish TV: https://www.dishtv.in/
  - Sun Direct: https://www.sundirect.in/
STEP 2: Request cancellation or transfer
STEP 3: Submit death certificate
STEP 4: Return set-top box for security deposit refund
- Timeline: 15-30 days for refund

GENERAL TIPS:
- Prioritize utilities linked to the property the family will continue using
- Transfer is generally easier and cheaper than closing and getting a new connection
- Always settle outstanding dues to avoid penalties or legal notices
- Keep copies of all cancellation/transfer acknowledgments
- Check for security deposits — many utility companies hold deposits that can be refunded
